experiments.name: Zone-2 volume block
experiments.hypothesis: Adding 60 min/wk of Zone-2 for 21 days lifts 7-day HRV average by >=5 ms without raising RHR.
experiments.mechanism: Aerobic base work shifts autonomic balance parasympathetic — mitochondrial density up, resting sympathetic tone down.

design.stopping_rule: Stop early only if RHR rises >4 bpm above baseline for 5 consecutive days.

experiments.name: Berberine — Glucose Attenuation
experiments.hypothesis: 500mg Berberine before high-carb meals will reduce CGM peaks above 140 mg/dL by ≥20%.
experiments.result_summary: Nature's metformin. The glucose data on this compound is hard to argue with.
experiments.evidence_citation: Yin et al., Metabolism 2008

experiments.name: Creatine Monohydrate — Strength
experiments.hypothesis: 5g daily creatine for 56 days will produce ≥8% increase in major lift 1RMs vs pre-supplementation baseline.
experiments.result_summary: The most evidence-backed supplement in existence. If you're lifting and not taking creatine, you're leaving gains on the table.
experiments.evidence_citation: Kreider et al., J Int Soc Sports Nutrition 2017

experiments.name: NMN — NAD+ Precursor Stack
experiments.hypothesis: 250mg NMN daily for 112 days will produce measurable improvement in HRV (≥8%) and subjective energy.
experiments.result_summary: NAD+ declines with age. If you can raise it back, the downstream effects touch everything from DNA repair to mitochondrial function.
experiments.evidence_citation: Yoshino et al., Science 2021

experiments.name: Tongkat Ali — Recovery Optimization
experiments.hypothesis: 400mg daily Tongkat Ali for 84 days will improve WHOOP recovery score by ≥5 points.
experiments.result_summary: One of the few non-prescription compounds with human trial data on testosterone and cortisol modulation.
experiments.evidence_citation: Talbott et al., J Int Soc Sports Nutrition 2013

experiments.name: Dairy Elimination
experiments.hypothesis: Removing dairy for 21 days then reintroducing will reveal whether dairy contributes to inflammation, digestive issues, or skin problems.
experiments.result_summary: You can't know if dairy affects you without removing it completely. The reintroduction phase is where the signal appears.
experiments.evidence_citation: Turnbull et al., Journal of the American College of Nutrition 2015

experiments.name: No Screens Before Bed
experiments.hypothesis: Eliminating screens 60 minutes before bed for 30 days will reduce subjective sleep onset latency and improve sleep consistency.
experiments.result_summary: The screen is the last thing most people see before sleep and the first thing they see after. Breaking that loop is the simplest sleep intervention that exists.
experiments.evidence_citation: Chang et al., PNAS 2015

experiments.name: Fixed Wake Time
experiments.hypothesis: Waking within ±15 min of {target_time} for 30 days will improve average HRV by ≥5% and sleep score by ≥3 points vs baseline.
experiments.result_summary: Your body doesn't know it's Saturday. The most powerful sleep hack isn't a supplement — it's consistency.
experiments.evidence_citation: Wittmann et al., Chronobiology International 2006

experiments.name: Creatine: Morning vs Evening
experiments.hypothesis: Switching creatine from evening to morning dosing will maintain cognitive benefits without disrupting sleep metrics.
experiments.result_summary: Creatine is one of the most studied supplements. Timing optimization is the next frontier after confirming the dose.
experiments.evidence_citation: Avgerinos et al., Experimental Gerontology 2018

experiments.name: Morning Mobility Routine
experiments.hypothesis: A consistent 10-minute morning mobility routine for 30 days will become automatic and improve subjective readiness.
experiments.result_summary: The body you wake up in isn't the body you had yesterday. Ten minutes of movement reminds your joints they exist.
experiments.evidence_citation: Behm & Chaouachi, European J Applied Physiology 2011

experiments.name: Dopamine Reset Weekend
experiments.hypothesis: One low-stimulation weekend per month for 30 days will improve weekday focus and reduce compulsive phone checking.
experiments.result_summary: Modern life is a firehose of dopamine. Periodic withdrawal teaches your brain to find satisfaction in ordinary experience.
experiments.evidence_citation: Lembke, Dopamine Nation 2021

experiments.name: 40g Protein at Breakfast
experiments.hypothesis: 40g protein at breakfast for 30 days will reduce total daily caloric intake and support weight loss trajectory.
experiments.result_summary: Front-loading protein changes the entire day. You eat less later without trying.
experiments.evidence_citation: Leidy et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2015

experiments.name: Breathwork Before Sleep
experiments.hypothesis: 5 minutes of 4-7-8 breathing before bed for 14 days will increase pre-sleep HRV and reduce sleep onset latency.
experiments.result_summary: Free. Takes 5 minutes. The extended exhale is a direct parasympathetic lever.
experiments.evidence_citation: Ma et al., Frontiers in Psychology 2017

experiments.name: L-Theanine for Focus
experiments.hypothesis: 200mg L-theanine with morning coffee for 14 days will improve subjective focus and reduce caffeine jitteriness.
experiments.result_summary: The caffeine smoothing agent. Same energy, less edge. The combo is better than either alone.
experiments.evidence_citation: Owen et al., Nutritional Neuroscience 2008

experiments.name: Vegetable Volume Loading
experiments.hypothesis: Adding 2 cups of vegetables to every meal for 30 days will reduce total caloric intake and support weight loss.
experiments.result_summary: You can't outeat two cups of broccoli. Volume is the satiety lever most people never pull.
experiments.evidence_citation: Rolls et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2004

experiments.name: Deep Work Block (2 Hours)
experiments.hypothesis: A daily 2-hour deep work block for 30 days will increase high-quality output and reduce perceived busyness.
experiments.result_summary: Two hours of real focus produces more than eight hours of multitasking. The problem isn't time — it's attention.
experiments.evidence_citation: Mark et al., CHI 2008; Newport, Deep Work 2016

experiments.name: Morning Sunlight + Evening Blue Blockers
experiments.hypothesis: Combined morning sunlight + evening blue blockers will reduce sleep onset latency and increase deep sleep %
experiments.result_summary: Full circadian protocol: morning light + evening protection
experiments.evidence_citation: Huberman Lab; Chang et al., PNAS 2015

experiments.name: Active Recovery Day Protocol
experiments.hypothesis: Replacing passive rest days with active recovery for 30 days will maintain recovery scores above baseline.
experiments.result_summary: Rest doesn't mean stillness. The body recovers faster when it moves — just not hard.
experiments.evidence_citation: Laukkanen et al., JAMA Internal Medicine 2015

experiments.name: Community Event Once Per Month
experiments.hypothesis: Attending one community event per month for 90 days will expand the outer social ring by ≥3 acquaintances.
experiments.result_summary: Male loneliness is an epidemic. The cure isn't more texting — it's showing up in a room with other people.
experiments.evidence_citation: Dunbar, How Many Friends Does One Person Need? 2010

experiments.name: Magnesium Glycinate for Sleep
experiments.hypothesis: 400mg magnesium glycinate before bed for 30 days will improve deep sleep % and reduce sleep onset latency.
experiments.result_summary: Magnesium is involved in 300+ enzymatic reactions. Most Americans are deficient. Sleep is where you notice it first.
experiments.evidence_citation: Abbasi et al., Journal of Research in Medical Sciences 2012

experiments.name: Morning Sunlight Exposure
experiments.hypothesis: 10 minutes of morning sunlight within 30 min of waking for 30 days will improve sleep onset consistency.
experiments.result_summary: Your eyes set your clock. The Pacific Northwest makes this harder — which makes it more important.
experiments.evidence_citation: Blume et al., Sleep Medicine Reviews 2019

experiments.name: No Caffeine After 12pm
experiments.hypothesis: Eliminating caffeine after 12pm for 30 days will reduce sleep latency and increase deep sleep % by ≥10%.
experiments.result_summary: That 2pm coffee costs you 30 minutes of deep sleep tonight. Most people don't feel it — the data shows it.
experiments.evidence_citation: Drake et al., J Clin Sleep Med 2013

experiments.name: No News/Social Media Before 10am
experiments.hypothesis: No news or social media before 10am for 14 days will improve morning focus and reduce reactive thinking.
experiments.result_summary: The first thing you consume sets the frame for the day. Other people's emergencies are not yours.
experiments.evidence_citation: Clinical consensus; Newport, Digital Minimalism 2019

experiments.name: Talk to a Stranger Daily
experiments.hypothesis: Daily stranger conversations for 14 days will increase social confidence and improve daily mood ratings.
experiments.result_summary: Social muscles atrophy. This is the gym for your social confidence. Most people find it's far less scary than expected.
experiments.evidence_citation: Epley & Schroeder, Journal of Experimental Psychology 2014

experiments.name: Vice Elimination Sprint
experiments.hypothesis: Eliminating one vice completely for 30 days will extend the vice streak and reveal dependency patterns.
experiments.result_summary: You don't know what something costs you until you stop paying. Pick the vice you're most afraid to quit — that's the one.
experiments.evidence_citation: Baumeister & Tierney, Willpower 2011

experiments.name: One Meaningful Conversation Per Day
experiments.hypothesis: One meaningful conversation daily for 30 days will shift social interaction quality measurably upward.
experiments.result_summary: Most days are filled with transactional words. One real exchange changes the texture of the entire day.
experiments.evidence_citation: Mehl et al., Psychological Science 2010

experiments.name: Post-Dinner Walk
experiments.hypothesis: Walking 10 minutes after dinner for 30 days will reduce post-meal glucose spikes by ≥20% vs baseline.
experiments.result_summary: The post-dinner walk is the most underrated intervention in metabolic health. Ten minutes. No gear. No app. Just gravity and glucose.
experiments.evidence_citation: Buffey et al., Sports Medicine 2022

experiments.name: Date Night Per Week
experiments.hypothesis: Weekly date nights for 60 days will improve relationship satisfaction and become a protected ritual.
experiments.result_summary: Proximity is not connection. A scheduled date night sounds unromantic — until it becomes the best night of the week.
experiments.evidence_citation: Dew & Wilcox, J Marriage & Family 2011

experiments.name: Fiber-First Meal Order
experiments.hypothesis: Eating fiber/vegetables before carbohydrates for 14 days will reduce average glucose spikes by >20%.
experiments.result_summary: Same food, different glucose response. This is the simplest dietary hack with the strongest CGM evidence.
experiments.evidence_citation: Shukla et al., Diabetes Care 2015

experiments.name: Call a Friend/Family Member Weekly
experiments.hypothesis: One phone call per week to a friend/family member for 30 days will deepen at least 2 relationships.
experiments.result_summary: Men are terrible at maintaining friendships. A weekly call is the minimum viable dose for not disappearing from people's lives.
experiments.evidence_citation: Holt-Lunstad et al., PLOS Medicine 2010; Kumar & Epley, J Experimental Psychology 2021

experiments.name: Morning Pages
experiments.hypothesis: 30 days of morning pages will reduce decision fatigue scores and improve subjective mental clarity.
experiments.result_summary: Externalizes the mental chatter that otherwise runs in background loops. Anecdotally transformative for many practitioners.
experiments.evidence_citation: Cameron, The Artist's Way 1992
